Parametric curves can be used with pre-defined functions and arithmetic operations. For example, inputc(3)returns the point at parameter position 3 on curvec.You can also place a point on a curve using toolPointor commandPoint. Since the endpointsaandbare dynamic
you can use slider variables as well (see toolSlider).
valid_example:
Parametric curves can be used with pre-defined functions and arithmetic operations. For example, inputc(3)returns the point at parameter position 3 on curvec.You can also place a point on a curve using toolPointor commandPoint. Since the endpointsaandbare dynamic
you can use slider variables as well (see toolSlider).

syntax_list: By default, the elements of this list are not shown in theGraphics View.Lists can also be used as arguments in list operations (mentioned further in this article) orList Commands.Lists must contain objects of the same type (e.g. lists of numbers, lists of points…​), otherwise lists operations or using lists as commands arguments might yield unreliable results.
valid_example: By default, the elements of this list are not shown in theGraphics View.Lists can also be used as arguments in list operations (mentioned further in this article) orList Commands.Lists must contain objects of the same type (e.g. lists of numbers, lists of points…​), otherwise lists operations or using lists as commands arguments might yield unreliable results.

syntax_list: Use theUndobutton to recover accidentally deleted object.Parts ofPenStrokecreated with thePen Toolcan be erased. Select this tool and move a pen or the finger over the part to erase (touch screens) or click and hold the left mouse button while dragging the pointer over the part of graph to erase (non-touch screens).See alsoDeletecommand.
valid_example: Use theUndobutton to recover accidentally deleted object.Parts ofPenStrokecreated with thePen Toolcan be erased. Select this tool and move a pen or the finger over the part to erase (touch screens) or click and hold the left mouse button while dragging the pointer over the part of graph to erase (non-touch screens).See alsoDeletecommand.

syntax_list: TransparentGIFandPNGfiles are supported, but for PNGs it is necessary to make sure that they have an alpha channel (for example if editing is made usingIrfanView, save using the PNGOUT plugin and chooseRGB+Alpha)Inserted images will be auxiliary objects by default.
valid_example: TransparentGIFandPNGfiles are supported, but for PNGs it is necessary to make sure that they have an alpha channel (for example if editing is made usingIrfanView, save using the PNGOUT plugin and chooseRGB+Alpha)Inserted images will be auxiliary objects by default.

syntax_list: If points areAandB, the line’s direction vector is(B - A)See alsoLinecommand.

syntax_list: Click (or tap in the mobile app) on a segment (or interval), straight line, polygon, conic section, function, or curve to create a point on the object.Click (or tap in the mobile app) on the intersection of two objects to create their intersection point (see alsoIntersecttool andIntersectcommand).See alsoPointcommand.
valid_example: Click (or tap in the mobile app) on a segment (or interval), straight line, polygon, conic section, function, or curve to create a point on the object.Click (or tap in the mobile app) on the intersection of two objects to create their intersection point (see alsoIntersecttool andIntersectcommand).See alsoPointcommand.
